title: Consistency Training Helps Stop Sycophancy and Jailbreaks

Tested methods for mitigating sycophancy: Bias-augmented Consistency Training (BCT) enforcing output invariance; Activation Consistency Training (ACT) enforcing internal activation invariance across prompt augmentations.

title: Reward Hacking as Equilibrium under Finite Evaluation

title: Expressing stigma and inappropriate responses prevents LLMs from safely replacing mental health providers.

title: System 2 Attention (is something you might need too)
Definition of sycophancy: Tendency of LLMs to incorporate user opinions or preferences into responses, prioritizing agreement over factual accuracy.

Tested methods for mitigating sycophancy: System 2 Attention (S2A): regenerating input context via LLM reasoning to remove irrelevant/opinion-laden content before final response generation.

title: Do VLMs Have a Moral Backbone? A Study on the Fragile Morality of Vision-Language Models

title: Sycophantic Chatbots Cause Delusional Spiraling, Even in Ideal Bayesians
Definition of sycophancy: A chatbot bias towards validating users' claims regardless of their accuracy, reinforcing beliefs rather than correcting them.

novel evaluation metrics: Formalized notion of delusional spiraling within a Bayesian user model, measuring confidence divergence toward outlandish beliefs.

title: Sycophancy Is Not One Thing: Causal Separation of Sycophantic Behaviors in LLMs
Definition of sycophancy: Excessive agreement with or flattery of the user, decomposed into sycophantic agreement and sycophantic praise distinct from genuine agreement.

How they are measuring sycophancy: Difference-in-means directions, activation additions, and subspace geometry to identify and separate sycophantic agreement vs. sycophantic praise vs. genuine agreement in latent space.
Language models tested: Multiple models across families and scales (specific names not provided in abstract)
Tested methods for mitigating sycophancy: Activation additions to independently amplify or suppress sycophantic agreement and sycophantic praise without affecting other behaviors.
